rulings.Case Title: JAMES ROSCOE VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 2, 2017, in Department 503, Line 1, Defendant Crane Co.' motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues nos. 1-4, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony (as to the internal packing in Crane valves) and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and the testimony of William McLean and Anthony Pantaleoni (Exhibits E, F, G) create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (internal packing) attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication issue no. 5 (punitive damages), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's evidence (Exhibits C, E, G, H, N, P and X) creates a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration, plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage and the Court cannot conclude that no reasonable jury could find plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) (Part 1 of 2) (Tentative ruling continued, see next entry.) = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ROSCOE VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Added To Calendar For The Entry Of The Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: (Tentative ruling, continued from previous entry) (Part 2 of 2) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. (End of tentative ruling.) = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LLOYD ET AL VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 2, 2017, in Department 503, Line 2, Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued to February 9, 2017 pursuant to San Francisco Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.7(B) for defendant's failure to lodge a courtesy copy of its moving papers prior to both the January 26, 2017 initial hearing date and the February 2, 2017 continued hearing date, despite the Court's mandate to do so. Defendant must deliver a courtesy copy of its moving papers to Department 503 by 4:00p.m. on February 2, 2017. If defendant fails to comply, the Court will deny its motion. =(503/CML)
